calendar.matters:
ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Trial
Off Calendar Sep-22-2021 Off Calendar
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting #1, Report, Fees
Off Calendar Oct-13-2021 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Konstantine Demiris, via ZOOM, for Matthew Kelly, Petitioner; Christina Gallagher-Nelson and Harlan Watkins, via ZOOM, for Conrad Donner and Charles McGettigan, via ZOOM, Co-Trustees.

The court begins by querying counsel regarding whether to expect Mr. Zabronsky's appearance today. Ms. Gallagher-Nelson does not believe that Mr. Zabronsky plans to appear as he signed the stipulated judgment filed with the court. The court then requests confirmation that Mr. Zabronsky is not objection to the accounting. Mr. Demiris reports that it is his understanding that there is no objection to the court granting the accounting pointing to Mr. Zabronsky's agreement to the stipulated judgment which includes approval of the Trustee's First Accounting. The court confirms with counsel that there is no request for 1% trustee's fees. The court then confirms with Mr. Demiris that his client's petition may be taken off calendar. The court also confirms with counsel that the trial date in this matter will be taken off calendar.

Matthew B. Kelly's Petition for Relief, etc., is taken off calendar. The Trial Date is taken off calendar. The Petition for Orders, etc., is submitted.

Judge: Hon. Joseph Quinn. Clerk: Robert Woods. Deputy: K. Saephan. Not reported.

Matthew B Kellys Petition For Relief For Breach Of Fiduciary Duty Surcharge And Suspension And Removal Of Co Trustees And Appointment Of Successor Trustee
Off Calendar Sep-22-2021 Off Calendar

Petition For Order Approving Settlement Agreement And Mutual Release
Off Calendar Sep-16-2021 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Konstantine Demiris, via ZOOM, for Matthew B. Kelly, Beneficiary; Andrew Zabronsky and Kristin Iversen, via ZOOM, for the Matthew Kelly Family Foundation, Petitioner; Christina Nelson, via ZOOM, for Conrad Donner and Charles McGettigan, Co-Trustees.

The court begins by advising counsel that it is more apparent that approval of settlements in trust matters is tricky and confirms with counsel that settlement terms are consistent with the trust instrument. Mr. Zabronsky reports that the only beneficiary of the residual trust is his client and that the trustees of the residual and charitable trusts are Mr. Donner and Mr. McGettigan. Ms. Nelson reports on negotiations and the plan moving forward including filing a stipulated judgment soon. The court then requests confirmation that this settlement agreement is not an amendment to the trust. Mr. Zabronsky provides oral testimony regarding the parties' positions in the settlement and the process that lead to settlement. The court advises that it appears as though it is modifying the trust which would supersede the previous order issued by the court and that the court will not issue an order overriding that order. Mr. Zabronsky confirms that the terms within the settlement agreement do not modify the trust.

After additional discussion, the Petition for Order Approving Settlement, etc., is submitted.

Judge: Hon. Joseph Quinn. Clerk: Robert Woods. Deputy: R. Rosado. Not reported.

Matthew B Kellys Petition For Relief For Breach Of Fiduciary Duty Surcharge And Suspension And Removal Of Co Trustees And Appointment Of Successor Trustee
Off Calendar Apr-20-2021 Continued To Jul-27-21 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Konstantine Demiris, via ZOOM, counsel for Matthew Kelly, Beneficiary; Christina Nelson, via ZOOM, and Harlan Watkins, via ZOOM, Co-Counsel for Conrad Donner and Charles McGettigan, Co-Trustees; Andrew Zabronsky, via ZOOM, counsel for the Matthew Kelly Foundation.

At the outset, the court questions counsel regarding the status of the matter. Mr. Demiris reports that the parties have been working diligently to narrow the issues and believes that the parties are close to settlement. After additional discussion, Matthew Kelly's Petition for Relief, etc; Petition for Orders, etc, are continued to July 27, 2021 at 9:00 am in Department 204. The Order of Status Hearing and Case Management Hearing are taken off calendar.

Judge: Hon. Joseph Quinn; Clerk: Robert Woods; Bailiff: Deputy Tauscher. Not Reported.

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Case Management Hearing
Off Calendar Apr-20-2021 Off Calendar

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Case Management Hearing
Off Calendar Mar-16-2021 Continued To Apr-20-21 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

ORDER OF STATUS HEARING
Off Calendar Mar-16-2021 Off Calendar
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Harlan Watkins and Christina Gallagher-Nelson appeared by zoom for Conrad Donner and Charles McGettagen (Co-Trustees).
Stacie Nelson Matthew Kelly and Mary Kelly (Beneficiaries).
Andrew Zabronsky appeared by zoom for The Matthew Kelly Family Foundation.

The Court noted that the matter had previously been continued because it did not receive a joint case management statement as ordered, that it noticed the parties of its intent to issue sanctions and asked why the Court should not issues sanctions in the amount of $1,350.00 to each party. Counsel apologized to the Court for not complying with the order to provide a joint case management statement, asked for forgiveness, stated that they have been communicating well, that they have made progress on a settlement agreement, and that they feel they have done better because of it. The Court informed the parties that their failure to be ready for trial and failure to follow orders from the Court could result in a forfeiture of party's interest. The Court also spoke of the importance of the parties providing information to assist the Court in determining a litigation management plan, stated that it does not want to see separate plans, that it takes Counsels' word that a lesson was learned, and they will comply moving forward, and that it will not issue sanctions. Ms. Nelson stated that the parties have circulated a settlement agreement, that they appear to be at an impasse with certain terms of the settlement which is a deal breaker and that her Clients just obtained trial Counsel (Konstantine Demiris). Ms. Nelson also stated that her Clients will be ready for trial on July 27th and that a substitution of Counsel will be filed. Ms. Zabronsky stated that the Chairman of the foundation received a call from Ms. Nelson's Clients exploring making the settlement agreement work, and that if it doesn't settle quickly, they will need to move on a fast track to get to trial. Ms. Gallagher-Nelson stated that this is the first she knows about them being at an impasse and that it is disappointing. The Court stated that it expects a robust joint case management statement in connection with the upcoming case management hearing, witness lists, exhibit lists, etc., and that it expects the parties to have a meaningful meet and confer. The case management hearing of April 6, 2021 is continued to April 20, 2021 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204. The Petition for Relief for Breach of Fiduciary Duty, etc., and the Petition for Orders Settling, Allowing, and Approving First Account and Report of Co-Trustees, etc. are also continued to April 20, 2021 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204. The order to appear is off calendar.

Judge: Joseph M. Quinn. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: K. Kabasares. Not reported.

Matthew B Kellys Petition For Relief For Breach Of Fiduciary Duty Surcharge And Suspension And Removal Of Co Trustees And Appointment Of Successor Trustee
Off Calendar Oct-27-2020 Continued To Jan-19-21 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Stacie Nelson appeared by zoom for Matthew Kelly (Beneficiary).
Andrew Zabronsky appeared by zoom for Kelly Family Foundation.
Harlan Watkins appeared by zoom and Christina Nelson appeared by telephone for and with Conrad Donner by zoom and for Charles McGettigan (Co-Trustees).

Stacie Nelson stated that they had mediation, but it was unsuccessful, that Mr. Kelly made a settlement proposal, that there was a counter offer, that she suggested going back to a second mediation to hammer out a deal, and that the Co-Trustees and the Foundation declined and that they will file an ex parte petition to remove the Co-Trustees due to bad behavior of the Co-Trustees. Mr. Watkins stated that his Clients would like a trial date. The Court informed the Parties that it is issuing a case management order and that the order requires filing of joint case management statements and filing of a joint trial plan. Case management hearings are set for January 19, 2021 and April 6, 2021 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204. The trial is set for July 27, 2021 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204. Matthew Kelly is required to serve a copy of the order on all Parties. The responsibility to initiate a meet and confer is also assigned to Matthew Kelly. Stacie Nelson is to monitor the Court's register of actions to obtain a copy of the order. The case management order and set dates are on both matters. The Court also informed the Parties that on July 27, 2021, it will make a determination as to whether the matter will remain in the Probate Department, or go out Dept. 206 for trial. The Petition for Relief for Breach of Fiduciary Duty, etc. and the Petition for Orders Settling, Allowing and Approving First Account and Report of C0-Trustees, etc. are continued to January 21, 2021 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.

Judge: Joseph M. Quinn. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: K. Kabasares. Not reported.

Matthew B Kellys Petition For Relief For Breach Of Fiduciary Duty Surcharge And Suspension And Removal Of Co Trustees And Appointment Of Successor Trustee
Off Calendar Feb-25-2020 Continued To May-26-20 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Stacie Nelson for Matthew Kelly (Benificiary/Son of Decedent).
Russell Roeca for and with Conrad Donner and for Chalres McGettigan (Co-Trustees).
Andrew Zabronski for The Matthew Kelly Family Foundation.
Edward Koplowitz in Propria Persona.

The Court questions whether the Parties have spoken and how they wish to proceed. Ms. Nelson stated that they have not spoken, that she has served some discovery, that discovery is still outstanding and suggested that they return in 90-days. Mr. Roeca stated that he concurs, that they are just getting started with discovery, that they are going to want to take some depositions, and suggested bifurcating the issue of the retroactive payment and the issue of whether this Beneficiary is contesting the language in the trust. Ms. Nelson informed the Court that she is going to be filing a law suit regarding misrepresentation about representation. The Petition for Relief for Breach of Fiduciary Duty, etc. and the Petition for Order Settling, Allowing and Approving First Account and Report of Co-Trustees, etc. are continued to May 26, 2020 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204. A joint status report is to be filed by May 19, 2020 indicating where things stand and what the Parties anticipate to accomplish at the May 26th hearing.

Judge: Peter J. Busch. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: K. Tauscher. Not reported.
